description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with three stages: build, test, and release, using the variables defined for API tokens, image tags, and project IDs. In the build stage add a build-sha job that runs on the quay.io/buildah/stable:latest image and executes `buildah login -u gitlab-ci-token -p $CI_JOB_TOKEN $CI_REGISTRY`, `buildah build --pull -t $IMAGE .`, and `buildah push $IMAGE`. In the test stage add a unit tests job on the $IMAGE image that runs `coverage run -m unittest test_trigger.py`, `coverage report -m --fail-under 85 trigger.py`, and `coverage html`, publishing the htmlcov directory as a weekly artifact, and add a series of integration test jobs (basic integration test, detch integration test, env variables & job output integration test, host & url integration test, issue 14 integration test, issue 20 integration test, manual pass integration test, manual play integration test, project name integration test, retry integration test, retry pid integration test) that all extend a .integration-test-template (using $IMAGE, limited to CI_PROJECT_PATH == "finestructure/pipeline-trigger") and run various `trigger` commands with the appropriate flags. In the release stage add a pages job that moves htmlcov to a public directory, publishes it as a year‑long artifact, and runs only on the master branch; also add release-latest and release-tag jobs on quay.io/buildah/stable:latest that use the shared .image_release script (`buildah login`, `buildah pull $IMAGE`, `buildah tag $IMAGE $RELEASE_IMAGE`, `buildah push $RELEASE_IMAGE`) with RELEASE_IMAGE set to $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:latest on main and to $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$CI_COMMIT_TAG on tags.
